channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Kenyan-Born US Army Officer Gladys Kipsang Promoted to Major After 13 Years of Service
item.description: Kenyans continue to shine abroad, and one remarkable story comes from Gladys Kipsang, a Kenyan-born officer in the United States Army. After serving diligently for 13 years, she has been officially pr...

item.title: Orlando Pirates Held to Draw, Title Decided in Final Match
item.description: Orlando Pirates faced Durban City at Orlando Amstel Arena in their penultimate PSL match of the 2025/26 season, hoping to secure their first league title in 15 years. Despite a vibrant atmosphere and ...

item.title: APC Concludes House of Reps Primaries in Katsina, Produces Elected and Consensus Candidates
item.description: The All Progressives Congress (APC) for Katsina State don complete their primaries for House of Representatives seats ahead of the 2027 elections. For Mani/Bindawa Federal Constituency, Hajiya Jamila ...

item.title: Pasay Police Chief Confirms Solo Police Vehicle Left Senate Early Morning
item.description: Pasay City Police Chief Colonel Joselito De Sesto confirmed that he was the passenger in the white police vehicle that left the Senate premises at around 3 a.m. on May 14, 2026. He clarified that the ...

item.title: Behind-the-Scenes Portraits of Shirley MacLaine Filming Irma la Douce in 1963
item.description: These evocative behind-the-scenes portraits capture actress Shirley MacLaine during the production of the 1963 classic film Irma la Douce, directed by Billy Wilder. MacLaine stars as Irma, a spirited ...

item.title: Genus Group secures $200m equity to acquire MPC Kinetic
item.description: Genus Group, a rapidly growing infrastructure company based in Western Australia and a key player in the nation’s energy transition, has quietly raised equity to finance the acquisition of private equ...

item.title: Semenyo’s Brilliant Back-Flick Secures Manchester City FA Cup Victory Over Chelsea
item.description: For Saturday wey just pass, Antoine Semenyo show proper skill as e score one kind magnificent back-flick wey settle Manchester City 1-0 win against Chelsea for FA Cup final for Wembley. This goal na t...

item.title: Semenyo’s decisive goal earns Manchester City FA Cup victory over Chelsea
item.description: Manchester City secured a 1-0 victory over Chelsea in the FA Cup final at Wembley, thanks to a moment of brilliance from Antoine Semenyo in the second half. In a tightly contested match with few clear...

item.title: Suspected Drunk Driver to Face Charges After Reckless Crash Injures Motorcyclist
item.description: Authorities in Klang, Malaysia, are set to formally charge a man suspected of driving under the influence after a serious traffic incident left a motorcyclist critically injured. The suspect, a man in...

item.title: Kericho Family Mourns Loss of Nurse Daughter Who Passed After Short Illness
item.description: A family in Ainamoi, Kapkwen Village, Kipchimchim, Kericho County is grieving after losing their beloved daughter, Mercy Chepkirui, who worked as a nurse in Nairobi. Mercy tragically passed away on Th...

item.title: Nigerian Airlines Review Business Models Amid Fuel Crisis and Global Opportunities
item.description: Nigerian airlines dey plan to rework their business strategies because recent increase for aviation fuel don put plenty pressure on dem. Companies like Ibom Air, Air Peace, and Rano Air dey consider c...

item.title: ICC sets proposed trial date for Rodrigo Duterte, defense raises health concerns
item.description: Prosecutors at the International Criminal Court (ICC) have proposed November 30, 2026, as the start date for the trial of former Philippine President Rodrigo Duterte, who faces charges of crimes again...

item.title: Bannu Police Neutralise Five Militants in Intelligence-Led Operation
item.description: In Bannu district, police have successfully conducted a major operation named 'Operation Intiqam-e-Shuhada' or 'Operation Revenge of the Martyrs', resulting in the elimination of five militants. This ...

item.title: ACT Government to Enforce Parking Rules at Local Sports Grounds Amid Facility Concerns
item.description: Canberra parents and sports clubs are facing a tough weekend as the ACT government launches a targeted parking enforcement blitz at major local sports grounds. Grounds such as Kaleen, Harrison, O’Conn...

item.title: Increasing Numbers of Canadian Jews Relocate Abroad Amid Rising Antisemitism
item.description: In recent years, a growing number of Canadian Jews have been leaving the country, primarily relocating to Israel and the United States, driven by concerns about rising antisemitism. According to the I...

item.title: Kenya Meteorological Department Forecasts Rain and Strong Winds Across Several Counties
item.description: The Kenya Meteorological Department (KMD) has issued a weather forecast for the next 24 hours, highlighting scattered rainfall across multiple counties in Kenya. The heaviest showers are expected over...

item.title: 84-Year-Old Man Missing in Tampines Found Safe by Singapore Police
item.description: Singapore police announced on May 16, 2026, that an 84-year-old man reported missing the previous day has been located and is safe. Mr. U Swee, also known as Chang Yann Ching, was last seen around 2 p...

item.title: Bandi Sai Bhageerath, son of Union minister, surrenders for questioning in Pocso case
item.description: Bandi Sai Bhageerath, who is the son of a Union minister, surrendered before the police on Sunday following the issuance of a look-out circular against him in connection with a case under the Protecti...

item.title: B.C. Tribunal Dismisses Claim That Classical Liberal Beliefs Are Protected Political Views
item.description: A recent decision by the British Columbia Human Rights Tribunal dismissed a complaint from former political science professor Joshua Gordon, who alleged he was denied a tenure-track position at Simon ...

item.title: CBSE Implements Three-Language Policy for Classes 9 and 10 with Internal Assessment for Third Language
item.description: The Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE) has officially rolled out the three-language policy for students from Class 9 onwards, starting July 1, 2026. Under this policy, students must study thr...
